response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced full-stack developer with a long career spanning many technologies, but his profile is fundamentally misaligned with this role. The position demands deep Java/Kotlin/JVM expertise, hands-on Spring ecosystem experience, and a genuine focus on IDE internals and enterprise tooling — none of which are evident in his resume. His primary stack is TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Java appears only in passing references (robotic arms, some embedded tooling), with no mention of Spring, Quarkus, Hibernate, or any JVM-level work. His single IDE-adjacent role (NI LabWindows/CVI) was C-based, over two decades ago, and peripheral to core IDE development. The breadth of his career does not compensate for the absence of the specific technical depth this role requires.
strengths: Decades of engineering experience across complex, large-scale systems (e.g., Sociomantic handling millions of HTTP requests/sec)
Demonstrated interest in developer tooling and IDE-adjacent work (NI LabWindows/CVI, MCP code generators, TDD infrastructure)
Strong testing culture and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, talks, open-source testing libraries)
concerns: No meaningful Java or Kotlin depth — Java is listed as a skill but never features as a primary technology in any role
Zero Spring ecosystem experience — Spring, Quarkus, and Hibernate are completely absent from the resume
No genuine IDE internals work — the only IDE role was writing C/C++ components for a C IDE over 20 years ago, with no JVM or language-tooling relevance

key_factors.factor: No Spring or enterprise framework experience
Developer tooling interest and large-scale systems exposure
